_comment:
Scenario definitions consumed by `cargo run --example capture_traces` and `cargo test --test replay_traces`. Every scenario is a real prompt + tools setup that we send to every provider unless gated by `providers.<name>.skip`. Captures land in tests/cross_provider/traces/<provider>/<scenario>.{request.json,response.sse,meta.json}.

## Schema

- name: required, used as the file stem.
- description: human-readable purpose / what wire shape it pins.
- max_tokens, temperature: optional generation params.
- expect_failure: if true, a non-2xx response is the success case (the body is still saved so error-shape tests can replay it).
- providers.<openai|google|anthropic>: optional per-provider overrides:
    - skip: skip this provider entirely.
    - model: override the default model for this scenario.
    - auth_override: literal bearer token to send instead of the resolved one (useful for 401 captures — value never round-trips into the saved request body).
    - extra_body: object merged into the request body before sending (e.g. reasoning, thinkingConfig, parallel_tool_calls).
- messages: array of items. Each item is one of:
    - { role: system|user|assistant, content: "..." }
    - { role: assistant, content: "...", tool_calls: [{ id, name, arguments }] }   // assistant emitted these calls in a prior turn
    - { role: tool, tool_call_id: "...", content: "..." }                          // tool result for that call_id
- tools: optional list of { name, description, parameters }.

scenarios.name: text_only
scenarios.description: Single user prompt, expect a short free-form response. Smallest possible streaming round-trip. max_tokens leaves headroom for thinking-model overhead (Gemini 2.5 burns reasoning tokens before emitting output).

messages.content: Say the single word 'hello' and nothing else.

scenarios.name: system_and_user
scenarios.description: System instruction + user prompt — exercises role mapping and (for Vertex) systemInstruction routing.

messages.content: You are a terse parrot. Reply with exactly the word the user gives you, lowercased.

scenarios.name: function_call
scenarios.description: Prompt + one tool, expect the model to emit a function call. Locks in the tool-call wire shape.

messages.content: What's the weather in Paris? Use the get_weather tool.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

scenarios.name: multi_turn_tool
scenarios.description: Pre-populated history with a prior assistant tool call + tool result. Pins the wire shape we send back into each provider for function_call_output / functionResponse / tool_result, and the model's handling of an in-progress tool conversation.

messages.content: Let me check that for you.
Summarise it in one short sentence.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

scenarios.name: length_limit
scenarios.description: Small max_tokens against a long-answer prompt. Forces FinishReason::Length / response.incomplete on OpenAI and MAX_TOKENS on Gemini — wires we previously had no real example of. 16 is the OpenAI minimum for max_output_tokens (smaller bodies are rejected as 400).

messages.content: Write a detailed five-paragraph essay about the history of clouds.

scenarios.name: parallel_tools
scenarios.description: Ask for the weather in three cities in one turn. Most current models will batch this as multiple function calls in a single response — exercises the parallel function-call wire shape (multiple function_call output items on OpenAI, multiple functionCall parts on Gemini).

messages.content: What is the weather in Paris, London, and Tokyo right now? Call the tool once per city.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

scenarios.name: reasoning_request
scenarios.description: Request a reasoning model with effort=medium and a summary so we capture reasoning_summary_text deltas on the wire. OpenAI-only: Gemini 2.5 already exposes reasoning token *counts* via thoughtsTokenCount in the existing captures, but doesn't stream reasoning *text* by default and the lib doesn't yet parse thought parts.

messages.content: If a train leaves Paris at 3pm at 80 km/h heading toward Lyon (470 km away) and another leaves Lyon at 4pm at 100 km/h heading toward Paris, at what time and how far from Paris do they meet? Show your working briefly.

scenarios.name: auth_error
scenarios.description: Send a deliberately-bogus bearer token. Captures the real 4xx error envelope from each provider so the typed-error mapping has ground truth to test against.

scenarios.name: model_not_found
scenarios.description: Request a non-existent model. On OpenAI this is a 404 with `error.code = model_not_found`; on Vertex this is 404 with a structured google.rpc.Status body. Locks in the body shape so error-mapping tests can match against real responses.
